ddt:Target.Name: DCDC_ePT2020_v4.0
ddt:Target.Description: v4.0 Diag Hardening: - Vdiag $44 - Log. DC2H - SW C400 - DID $F062 added

Data.Name: SessionName
Data.Comment: Diagnostic session values. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
[0x00] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x01] : Default session
[0x02] : Programming session
[0x03] : Extended diagnostic session
[0x04] : Safety system diagnostic session
[0x05 - 0x3F] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x40 - 0x5F] : Vehicle manufacturer specific
[0x60 - 0x7E] : System supplier specific
[0x7F] : ISO / SAE Reserved

Data.Name: systemSupplierIdentifier
Data.Comment: Name and address information of the supplier.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier name and address information.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerKitAssemblyPartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer order number for a kit (assembled parts bought as a whole for production e.g. cockpit), when the spare part number designates only the server (e.g. for aftersales).

The record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: VIN
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the VIN number.

Record data content and format shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer.

It shall not be erased when the ECU is reprogrammed.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUHardwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used by reading services to reference the vehicle-manufacturer-specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be serverspecific and defined by vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: diagnosticVersion
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the diagnostic version of the ECU.

It is defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Refer to manufacture ECU designer.

Data.Name: VehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber_N
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by Nissan

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareVersionNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software version number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: TemperatureLVPath1
Data.Comment: Measured PCB temperature of path 1 on LV side

Data.Name: TemperatureLVPath2
Data.Comment: Measured PCB temperature of path 2 on LV side

Data.Name: Vdd13ProtSupplyVoltage
Data.Comment: Auxialiary power supply voltage for gate drivers

Data.Name: Supplier_Fault_Code
Data.Description: For each DTC is associated an internal fault code defined by the supplier ECU. This internal fault code is stored in DID $EF.01 and is used only for a snapshot. This DID is used by an ECU designer or an ECU supplier to know the root cause of the failure.

Data.Name: DesiredOpModePath1
Data.Comment: Internal desired operating mode for Dcdc converter path 1

Data.Name: DesiredOpModePath2
Data.Comment: Internal desired operating mode for Dcdc converter path 2

Data.Name: DetailedOpModePath1
Data.Comment: Internal detailed operation mode of first converter path

Data.Name: DetailedOpModePath2
Data.Comment: Internal detailed operation mode of second converter path

Data.Name: PrimaryTransfoCurrentPath1
Data.Comment: Primary transformer current of path 1 (HV side)

Data.Name: PrimaryTransfoCurrentPath2
Data.Comment: Primary transformer current of path 2 (HV side)

Data.Name: GlobalEnableStatePath1
Data.Comment: HW enable status of converter path 1

Data.Name: GlobalEnableStatePath2
Data.Comment: HW enable status of converter path 2

Data.Name: FingerPrint
Data.Description: The FingerPrint is defined by the OEM and it is used by the ECU using the Security Access for the reprogramming process or write secure DID.

Item.Text: Device and failure type ODB codding
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level out of range / zero adj. failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component or sys. operation obstructed or blocked
Input Supply Voltage Sensor/ECU Switch Off Failure
LV Rectifier Voltage Sensor Phase 1 Failure
LV Rectifier Voltage Sensor Phase 2 Failure
Read Error In Non Volatile Memory
Write Error In Non Volatile Memory
Reverse Current HW Latch Reset Is Invalid
LV Overvoltage Detected On Boardnet Side

Data.Name: DTCOccurenceCounter
Data.Comment: Number of occurence failure which has been detected

Data.Name: DCDC_Input_Current
Data.Comment: DCDC internal measurement of input HV current

Data.Name: DCDC_Output_Voltage_2
Data.Comment: DCDC output voltage measured by sensor 2 (redundant)

Data.Name: DCDC_Output_Voltage
Data.Comment: DCDC output voltage measured by sensor 1

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Transmitted_DCDC_ElecSysFailureDisplay
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDC_ElecSysFailureDisplay transmitted from DCDC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Transmitted_DCDC_Fault_Type
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDC_Fault_Type transmitted from DCDC

Item.Text: Fault type 1 DCDC Stopped CLASS C
Fault type 2 DCDC Stopped CLASS D
Fault type 4 DCDC Safety Barrier lost

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Transmitted_DCDC_ElecMotorFailureDisplay
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDC_ElecMotorFailureDisplay transmitted from DCDC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Transmitted_MaxCurrentAvailableBySystem
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal MaxCurrentAvailableBySystem transmitted from DCDC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Transmitted_DCDCTemp
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDCTemp transmitted from DCDC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Transmitted_DCDC_RefusetoSleep
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDC_RefusetoSleep transmitted from DCDC

Data.Name: CurrentLVPath1
Data.Comment: Measured current of path 1 on LV side

Data.Name: CurrentLVPath2
Data.Comment: Measured current of path 2 on LV side

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_DistanceTotalizer
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DistanceTotalizer received from HEVC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_GADE_HV_v2
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal GADE_HV_v2 received from HEVC

Item.Text: Sleep or Crank fault, Disable DiagMuxDTC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_DCDCVoltageRegulation_v2
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDCVoltageRegulation_v2 received from HEVC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_WakeUpSleepCommand
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal WakeUpSleepCommand received from HEVC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Transmitted_DCDCInputPower
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDCInputPower transmitted from DCDC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Transmitted_DCDCCurrentOutput
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDCCurrentOutput transmitted from DCDC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_VehicleSpeed
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal VehicleSpeed received from HEVC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_VehicleStates
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal VehicleStates_HV received from HEVC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_HVFOTAConditionCheckStatus
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal HVFOTAConditionCheckStatus_HV received from HEVC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Transmitted_DCDCInterlockState
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDCInterlockState transmitted from DCDC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Transmitted_DCDCState
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDCState transmitted from DCDC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_BatteryVoltage
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal BatteryVoltage received from HEVC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_DCDCActivation
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal DCDCActivation received from HEVC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_BMS_InterlockServicePlug
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal BMS_InterlockServicePlug received from HEVC

Data.Name: DCDC_CAN_Received_AbsoluteTimeSince1rstIgnition
Data.Comment: Copy of CAN signal AbsoluteTimeSince1rstIgn received from HEVC

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.AgingCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData AgingCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCOccurrenceCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCFormatIdentifier
Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Data.Name: ECUSerialNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Name: MeasuredHVCurrentInput
Data.Comment: Measured HV current by Total Current Sensor

Data.Name: HVOverCurrentThreshold
Data.Comment: HV overcurrent detection threshold for TCS

Data.Name: ErrorSafeStateReqHardware
Data.Comment: HW circuit causing safe-state of DCDC (bit-coded)

Data.Name: ErrorSafeStateReqSoftware
Data.Comment: SW function causing safe-state of DCDC (bit-coded)

Data.Name: ErrrorCounterWatchdogQueryExternal
Data.Comment: error counter of microcontroller with external watchdog (query-response error)

Data.Name: ErrorCounterWatchdogSPIExternal
Data.Comment: error counter of microcontroller with external watchdog (spi error)

Data.Name: CoreIdMPUTrap
Data.Comment: Microcontroller ID that caused the MPU trap

Data.Name: MemAccessMPUTrap
Data.Comment: Address of memory whose access caused MPU trap

Data.Name: MasterIdMPUTrap
Data.Comment: ID of bus-master which caused MPU trap

Data.Name: ProgAddressMPUTrap
Data.Comment: Program Adress which caused MPU trap

Data.Name: SwitchoverStatus
Data.Comment: This DID will be used by Bosch to check the current switchover status

Data.Name: SwitchoverGetSeed_WriteKey
Data.Comment: This DID will be used by Bosch to get the seed from DCDC and write the key back to DCDC while perfor

Data.Name: SwitchoverRemoveSwitchover
Data.Comment: This DID will be used by Bosch to remove the "switchover"

Data.Name: FOTAStateOfDCDC
Data.Comment: This DID returns the current state of FOTA state-machine in DCDC

Data.Name: FOTAResumeAddress
Data.Comment: This DID returns the address from which data transfer has to be resumed during "Installation" phase

Data.Name: FOTAFlashMemoryBankNumber
Data.Comment: This DID returns the memory bank number (current) used for FOTA update.

Data.Name: LvShortCircuitCountPath1
Data.Comment: number of detected short circuits on LV side of path A

Data.Name: LvShortCircuitCountPath2
Data.Comment: number of detected short circuits on LV side of path B

Data.Name: OutputCurrentCalculatedPath1
Data.Comment: output current calculated from primary transformer current of path A

Data.Name: OutputCurrentCalculatedPath2
Data.Comment: output current calculated from primary transformer current of path B

Data.Name: OutputVoltageMonitoringSW
Data.Comment: voltage measured from ULV1 by Level-2 software (monitoring)

Data.Name: OutputVoltageRedundantMonitoringSW
Data.Comment: voltage measured from ULV2 by Level-2 software (monitoring)

Data.Name: HVRangeCheckErrorCounterMonitoringSW
Data.Comment: Range check error counter for HV input voltage in L2-software (monitoring)

Data.Name: InputVoltageQualityMonitoringSW
Data.Comment: Quality of HV voltage measured by Level-2 software (monitoring)

Data.Name: InputVoltageMonitoringSW
Data.Comment: HV voltage measured by Level-2 software (monitoring)

Data.Name: CoolantTemperatureCalculated2
Data.Comment: coolant temperature estimated from the other converter path

Data.Name: CoolantTemperatureCalculated
Data.Comment: coolant temperature estimated from hottest converter path

Data.Name: SupplyVoltageKL30
Data.Comment: Internal supply voltage derived from B+ terminal

Data.Name: InputVoltageCalculatedFromOutputVoltage
Data.Comment: HV voltage calculated from LV voltage

Item.Text: Simulated power-on reset from bootloader (during/after re-programming)
Reset to jump to bootloader for reprogramming
Simulated power-on reset from SW when wake-up reason is detected in after-run (post-drive) phase
Simulated power-on reset from SW when a wake-up event is detected in shut-down phase
Simulated power-on reset from SW on receiving request from HEVC during a CLASS-D failure
Simulated power-on reset from bootloader (during/after re-programming)
Reset to jump to bootloader for reprogramming
Simulated power-on reset from SW when wake-up reason is detected in after-run (post-drive) phase
Simulated power-on reset from SW when a wake-up event is detected in shut-down phase
Simulated power-on reset from SW on receiving request from HEVC during a CLASS-D failure
Simulated power-on reset from bootloader (during/after re-programming)

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by Renault
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Request.Name: TesterPresent.WithResponse
Request.Comment: This request enable P2P tester present for tool (Screen, IOctl, ...)

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.ExtendedDiagnostic
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Default
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Programming
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: ClearDiagnosticInformation.All
Request.Comment: The ClearDiagnosticInformation service is used by the tool to clear diagnostic information in an ECUs memory

Request.Name: ReportDTCInformation.ReportDTCFaultDetectionCounter_CPDD Import
Request.Comment: Report DTC fault detection counter counter for Clip import

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportSupportedDTC_CPDD Import
Request.Comment: Report supported DTC for Clip import

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportExtendedData
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000 nor for Clip import

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportExtendedData.DTCOccurrenceCounter
Request.Comment: Request for the import in the Clip

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportExtendedData.AgingCounter
Request.Comment: Request for the import in the Clip

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportExtendedData.Mileage
Request.Comment: Request for the import in the Clip

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportSnapshot
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportDTC
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / DDT : Unusable for Clip import

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportDTC_CPDD Import
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT

Request.Name: DataRead.vehicleManufacturerECUSoftwareNumber
Request.Comment: Read Configuration file reference link RUC

Request.Name: DataRead.MeasuredHVCurrentInput
Request.Comment: Measured HV current by Total Current Sensor

Request.Name: DataRead.InputVoltageCalculatedFromOutputVoltage
Request.Comment: HV voltage calculated from LV voltage

Request.Name: DataRead.SupplyVoltageKL30
Request.Comment: Internal supply voltage derived from B+ terminal

Test.Description: HV Total current sensor Signal I< -2.5A
HV Total current sensor Signal I>20A
Multiple electrical fuse events happened on HV
Analog setpoint threshold of HV current is not plausible
Total current sensor is not plausible to the calculated HV current
Communication failure with temperature sensor phase 1
Temperature sensor phase 1 is out of range low (T1<-50.0°C)
Temperature sensor phase 1 is out of range (T1>150.0°C)
Shut-off due to hot-spot-temperature of phase 1 (T1>145°C)
Communication failure with temperature sensor phase 2
Temperature sensor phase 2 is out of range low (T2<-50.0°C)
Temperature sensor phase 2 is out of range high (T2>150.0°C)
Shut-off due to hot-spot-temperature of phase 2 (T2>145°C)
ULV sensor defect shown by Test-Pulse
Rationality (Diff>0.6V ULV/ULV2 and ULV or ULV2 > =15.7V) SW-L2 -> Stop
Rationality (Diff>0.6V ULV/ULV2 and ULV or ULV2 > =15.7V) -> Stop
Rationality (Diff>0.6V ULV/ULV2 and ULV & ULV2 <15.7V)SW-L2"for safety-barrier only"
Rationality (Diff>6V ULV/ULV2 and ULV & ULV2 <15.7V) "for safety-barrier only"

Device.Name: LV Rectifier Voltage Sensor Phase 1 Failure
LV Rectifier Voltage Sensor Phase 2 Failure

Test.Description: Low performance due to internal failure

Device.Name: Read Error In Non Volatile Memory
Write Error In Non Volatile Memory

Test.Description: Undervoltage on HV side detected UHV<215V
Overvoltage on HV side detected UHV>462.5V
Overcurrent on HV side phase 1 detected IHV1>15.5A

Device.Name: Reverse Current HW Latch Reset Is Invalid

Test.Description: HW latch for reverse current detection is defect

Device.Name: LV Overvoltage Detected On Boardnet Side

Test.Description: Shut off path test of phase 1 failed
Shut off path test of phase 2 failed
Redundant LV voltage measurement is defect

Device.Name: Input Supply Voltage Sensor/ECU Switch Off Failure

Test.Description: ECU Shut Off not possible or too Late
Overcurrent on HV side phase 2 detected IHV2>15.5A
DCDC_RN104 frame is not transmitted first
Too many not confirmed short circuit failures in phase 2
Too many not confirmed short circuit failures in phase 1
